# Caller-keyed Chroma write-guard (config-independent test-isolation)

## Context

The #14022 physical Chroma test-isolation (separate daemon + data dir under `UNIT_TEST_MODE`) is the right primary containment, but it is **config-resolution-dependent**: during the #14022 review, running the slice with a stale local config overlay made the active `engines.chroma.dataDir` resolve to the **production** path under `UNIT_TEST_MODE` â€” i.e. isolation silently fell back to prod coords. The same exposure applies to any run that doesn't load the unit config (a bare `npx playwright` â€” the documented 2026-05-17 wipe vector).

## The Problem

Config-driven isolation is only as strong as config resolution. When it goes wrong (stale overlay, missed migration, no-config run), unit-test Chroma writes silently target the live store â€” the exact bleed class. The graph (SQLite) and concept-ontology paths already have a **config-independent** caller-keyed guard; the Chroma write path does not.

## The Architectural Reality

`assertTestWriteIsolated` (fires on `TEST_WORKER_INDEX` â€” set by Playwright per worker â€” regardless of `UNIT_TEST_MODE`/config) is wired into:
- `ai/graph/storage/SQLite.mjs` (5 write funnels, #13639)
- `ai/services/ingestion/ConceptDiscoveryService.mjs` via `ai/services/shared/storeWriteGuard.mjs` (#13683)

It is **NOT** wired into the Chroma write path. The destructive-delete guard (#11652 `chromaDeleteCollection` / `ChromaManager.guardedDeleteCollection`) covers deletes-by-target, but additive writes have no caller-keyed gate.

## The Fix

Add a caller-keyed `assertTestWriteIsolated`-equivalent to the Chroma write path: when a test runner is detected (`TEST_WORKER_INDEX` / `UNIT_TEST_MODE`) AND the resolved Chroma coords are production-like, **throw** before the write â€” config-independent, fires regardless of overlay/config state. Symmetric to #13639 / #13683.

## Re-scope (PR #14044 cycle-1/N review â€” the broad caller-keyed coord predicate is UNACHIEVABLE)

The original "block any test-caller + production-like coords" predicate (above) is **not achievable** at the Chroma write/coordinate layer â€” proven during the #14044 review, cross-confirmed by @neo-gpt's coordinate probe. A fresh-workspace / cloud orchestrator daemon is a **legitimate** test-caller context: it inherits Playwright's `TEST_WORKER_INDEX` and resolves the *full production coordinate tuple* (`dataDirProd` is cwd-independent, so host/port/dataDir/database all = prod) while writing **production-named** collections (`neo-agent-memory`). It is byte-indistinguishable from a bleed at the coord layer; a caller-keyed coord guard crashes it at boot (the cycle-1 `integration-unified` regression).

The **achievable** discriminator is the **collection NAME**: the #14010 bleed creates `test-*` collections (`test-memory-*` / `test-session-*`) in the production database; a legitimate prod-coordinate daemon creates `neo-*` collections. So the guard fails closed on a `test-*` name resolving into the production database. The broad "prod-named write under a test caller" class is covered by **#14022 physical isolation** (separate daemon/dataDir prevents a correctly-configured test from reaching prod coords), not a coord guard â€” there is no achievable coord-level guard for it, so it is NOT a permanently-open AC here.

## Acceptance Criteria (revised â€” achievable guard)

- [ ] A `test-*`-named collection (`test-memory-*` / `test-session-*`) resolving into the production database (`default_database`) **fails closed** â€” the #14010 bleed signature, at the collection-creation boundary.
- [ ] No fresh-workspace false-positive: `neo-*`-named collections in production coordinates **proceed** (the legitimate cloud/fresh-workspace daemon â€” inherited `TEST_WORKER_INDEX` must not crash it).
- [ ] Unit coverage: `test-*` + prod DB rejected; `neo-*` + prod DB allowed; `test-*` + test DB allowed.
- [ ] The broad "prod-named write under a test caller" class is documented as covered by #14022 physical isolation (not this name guard).

## Out of Scope

- The #14022 physical isolation (this is its config-independent backstop, fast-follow sibling â€” not a replacement).
- The "tests must always use config.template" issue (separately ticketed per operator).

## Related

- #14022 â€” physical isolation (this backstops its fallback case).
- #13639 â€” the SQLite caller-keyed guard this mirrors.
- #13683 â€” the concept-ontology caller-keyed guard.
- #14010 â€” the test-bleed regression.

- 2026-06-26T03:02:03Z @neo-opus-vega referenced in commit `0eeb4ca` - "fix(ai): move Chroma test-write guard to the collection boundary (#14031)

Addresses the #14044 review: the resolver-level guard keyed on the production
database NAME over-fired. A fresh-workspace/cloud daemon legitimately uses
default_database in a cwd-isolated store and inherits Playwright's
TEST_WORKER_INDEX, so the guard crashed it at boot (the integration-unified
workspaceSafety path). The db-name is not the production COORDINATE: dataDirProd
is cwd-independent, so a fresh-workspace daemon resolves the full prod tuple
(host/port/dataDir/database) identical to the bleed â€” confirmed by the reviewer's
coordinate probe (install-path).

The discriminator the resolver lacked is the COLLECTION NAME: the bleed creates
test-* collections (test-memory-*/test-session-*) in the prod database, while a
legitimate prod-coordinate daemon creates neo-* collections. Move the guard to
the collection-creation boundary (assertCollectionNotProdBleed, called in the
three collection accessors): a test-* name resolving into the production database
fails closed; neo-* names never trip it. Removed the resolver-level guard and its
isTestRunnerContext import."
- 2026-06-26T07:32:34Z @tobiu referenced in commit `a3aae2a` - "fix(ai): config-independent Chroma test-write guard (#14031) (#14044)

* fix(ai): config-independent Chroma test-write guard (#14031)

The config-keyed test-isolation toggle (useTestDatabase) only fires when a
caller sets UNIT_TEST_MODE. A bare `npx playwright` run sets TEST_WORKER_INDEX
but never UNIT_TEST_MODE, so the toggle stays off and the resolver returns the
production database â€” the exact bleed that put test-memory/test-session
collections into the live namespace (#14010).

Add a config-INDEPENDENT fail-closed guard to ChromaManager.resolveChromaClientConfig,
symmetric to the SQLite-graph and concept-ontology guards in storeWriteGuard:
when isTestRunnerContext() is true (TEST_WORKER_INDEX or UNIT_TEST_MODE) and the
resolved database is the production namespace (CHROMA_PRODUCTION_DATABASE), throw
before any client targets it â€” regardless of config resolution.

The guard keys off the stable production-namespace constant, not the config leaf,
so a custom non-production database under the same runner is not a false-positive.

* fix(ai): move Chroma test-write guard to the collection boundary (#14031)

Addresses the #14044 review: the resolver-level guard keyed on the production
database NAME over-fired. A fresh-workspace/cloud daemon legitimately uses
default_database in a cwd-isolated store and inherits Playwright's
TEST_WORKER_INDEX, so the guard crashed it at boot (the integration-unified
workspaceSafety path). The db-name is not the production COORDINATE: dataDirProd
is cwd-independent, so a fresh-workspace daemon resolves the full prod tuple
(host/port/dataDir/database) identical to the bleed â€” confirmed by the reviewer's
coordinate probe (install-path).

The discriminator the resolver lacked is the COLLECTION NAME: the bleed creates
test-* collections (test-memory-*/test-session-*) in the prod database, while a
legitimate prod-coordinate daemon creates neo-* collections. Move the guard to
the collection-creation boundary (assertCollectionNotProdBleed, called in the
three collection accessors): a test-* name resolving into the production database
fails closed; neo-* names never trip it. Removed the resolver-level guard and its
isTestRunnerContext import."
- 2026-06-26T07:32:34Z @tobiu closed this issue
